1.1: PAUL, a servant of God, and an apostle of Jesus Christ, in the faith of God’s elect, and in the knowledge of true godliness;
1.2: In the hope of eternal life, which the true God promised ages ago;
1.3: And has in due time revealed his word by our preaching; which preaching has been intrusted to me by the command of God our Saviour;
1.4: To Titus, a true son in the common faith: Grace and peace from God the Father, and the LORD Jesus Christ our Saviour.
1.5: For this cause I left you in Crete, that you should set in order the things that are wanting and ordain elders in every city where there is a need as I had commanded you.
1.6: Appoint only an elder who is blameless, and the husband of one wife, and one who has faithful children who do not swear and who are not intemperate.
1.7: For an elder must be blameless, as a steward of God; and he must not be self-willed, not quick tempered, not excessive in the use of wine, not too ready to strike with his hand, not a lover of filthy lucre;
1.8: But a lover of hospitality, a lover of good things, sober, just, pious, and temperate of worldly desires;
1.9: Holding fast the doctrine of faith, so that he may be able to comfort by his sound doctrine, and to rebuke those who are proud.
1.10: For there are many unruly and vain talkers and deceivers of the people, especially those who belong to the circumcision:
1.11: Whose mouths must be stopped, for they corrupt many families, teaching things which they ought not, for the sake of filthy lucre.
1.12: One of them, even a prophet of their own, said, The Cre’tians are always liars, vicious beasts with empty bellies.
1.13: This testimony is true. Therefore rebuke them sharply, that they may be sound in the faith;
1.14: And not give heed to Jewish fables, and commandments of men who hate the truth.
1.15: To the pure, all things are pure: but nothing is pure to those who are defiled and faithless; even their mind and conscience is defiled.
1.16: They profess to know God, but in works they deny him, and they are abominable, and disobedient, condemning every kind of good work.

2.1: BUT you must preach the things which are proper to sound doctrine.
2.2: Teach the older men to be vigilant, sober, pure, sound in faith, in love, charity and patience.
2.3: Teach the older women likewise, to behave as becomes the worship of God, not false accusers, not enslaved to much wine, but to become teachers of good things;
2.4: That they may teach the young women to be modest, to love their husbands and their children,
2.5: To be discreet, chaste, good home keepers, obedient to their own husbands, so that no one can reproach the word of God.
2.6: Likewise exhort the young to be modest.
2.7: In every thing show yourself an example in all good works, and in your teaching let your word be sound.
2.8: Choose sound words that are instructive so that no man can point the finger of scorn at us, and so that he who is against us may be shamed, when he can find nothing evil to say about us.
2.9: Exhort servants to be obedient to their own masters and to please them well in all things not contentious;
2.10: Not stealing but manifesting true sincerity that they may adorn the doctrine of God our Saviour in all things.
2.11: For the grace of God that brings salvation has been revealed to all men.
2.12: It teaches us to renounce ungodliness and worldly lusts, and to live in this world soberly, righteously, and in godliness;
2.13: Looking for that blessed hope, and the glorious appearing of the great God and our Saviour Jesus Christ;
2.14: Who gave himself for us, that he might redeem us from all iniquity, and might purify us to be his own, a new people, zealous of good works.
2.15: These things speak and exhort and rebuke with all authority. Let no man despise you.

3.1: REMIND all to be obedient and submissive to princes and governors, and to be ready for every good work,
3.2: And not to speak evil against any man, and not to be quarrelsome, but to be meek, in every respect showing gentleness to all men.
3.3: For we ourselves also were sometimes foolish, disobedient, misled, and serving divers lusts and passions, living in malice and envy, hated, and also hating one another.
3.4: But after the goodness and kindness of God our Saviour was manifested,
3.5: Not so much by works of righteousness which we have done, but according to his mercy, he saved us by the washing of regeneration, and renewing of the Holy Spirit;
3.6: Which he shed on us abundantly, through Jesus Christ our Saviour,
3.7: That being justified by his grace, we should be made heirs to the hope of eternal life.
3.8: This is a true saying, and these things I want you to constantly affirm, so that those who believe in God may be careful to continually do good works. These things are good and profitable to men.
3.9: But avoid foolish questions, and genealogies, and contentions, and the theological arguments of the scribes, for they are unprofitable and vain.
3.10: After you have admonished the heretic once or twice shun him;
3.11: Knowing that he who is such is corrupt; he sins and condemns himself.
3.12: When I send Ar’te-mas or Tych’i-cus to you, endeavor to come to me at Ni-cop’olis: for I have decided to winter there.
3.13: See that Ze’nas, the scribe, and A-pol’los are given a good farewell on their journey, that they lack nothing.
3.14: And let our people be taught to do good works in times of emergency, that they be not unfruitful.
3.15: All who are with me salute you. Greet those who love us in the faith. Grace be with you all. Amen.
